title: Flatmate discussion
explanation:
Flatmate discussion

Listen to two flatmates talking about household rules.

Transcript

Ella: We need to talk about the cleaning rota. I feel like I have been doing most of the kitchen this month.
Tom: You are right. I have been busy, but that is not an excuse. How about we make a proper schedule?
Ella: Good idea. And could we also agree not to invite friends over on weeknights without asking first?
Tom: Fair enough. That sounds reasonable.

questions.text: Ella says she has been doing most of the [*kitchen cleaning|shopping|rent payments].

questions.feedback: He says he has been busy.

questions.text: They decide to make a [*schedule|phone call|complaint].
Ella wants flatmates to ask before inviting [*friends over on weeknights|family for weekends|repair workers].
questions.feedback: That is the second issue she raises.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The topic is the cleaning ____.  | *rota | campaign | bargain
questions.feedback: A rota is a schedule of duties.

questions.text: Tom says being busy is not an ____.  | *excuse | update | argument
questions.feedback: He calls it not an excuse.

questions.text: Ella thinks the new rule sounds ____.  | *reasonable | impossible | unfair
They are discussing life in a shared ____.  | *flat | office | hotel
The conversation is about [*shared responsibilities|travel plans|exam stress].
questions.feedback: Cleaning and visitors in a shared home.

questions.text: Tom reacts in a [*cooperative|defensive|aggressive] way overall.
questions.feedback: He agrees and suggests a solution.
She mentions the kitchen and weeknights.

questions.text: The tone becomes more [*constructive|hostile|formal] by the end.
